knowledge.insight: Johnston: On the St. Lawrence River, sonar doesn't always mark bass on drop-offs, which are key spots where bass push bait. River bass sit very tight to bottom. The Aqua-Vu HD10i Pro with on-screen water temp and depth, using the XD Trolling Fin and extra XD weight, gets down to depth and provides a stable picture of what's happening.

Johnston: Drop-offs are the key structure on the St. Lawrence because bass can push bait up against them. Sonar won't mark these tight-to-bottom fish, making an underwater camera essential for confirming fish presence.
